title: the interface headset and kaworus sync with mark06
display_title: the interface headset and kaworus sync with mark06
wikitext:
[]: the interface headset and kaworu's sync with mark.06             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jul 26, 2010 1:16 pm ok, so i was taking clips of 2.22 yesterday, and all of a sudden i noticed something i had missed the first eight-or-so times i watch the film (yes, yes, i'm slow) : in the very last scene of the movie (unit-01's impalement), kaworu doesn't seem to be wearing an interface headset. now, even in his Shinji's very first battle, he wore the headset. not the plugsuit, but he had the headset. even with all the rush they were in, the desperation of the situation which would make you think they wouldn't be focusing on little details like, say, Shinji knowing how to pilot the giant robot, they were careful enough to have him wear the headset. so, clearly, the headset is important for the average pilot. kaworu is very likely to be... special. again. i think most of us are comfortable with the assumption that he'll be an angel. again. so it probably makes sense to us that he wouldn't need the headset to enhance synchronization with the eva-- he probably can adjust the synchronization at will, like he did with unit-02 in the series. but what i'm really curious about is... are SEELE not even bothering to hide it? were they counting on the fact that nobody would be able to see kaworu inside mark.06, just like nobody saw mari ( yet? ) inside unit-02? what if they take kaworu in for questioning after (like i'm assuming they will do with mari)? surely someone, likely ritsuko, could notice the conspicuous absence of the headset. for that matter, is anno not even bothering to hide it? it wasn't exactly a long-standing secret in the series, of course (just one episode and only the last part of it had anything to do with the synchronization with the eva) . i guess it would be a pretty easy detail to miss for someone who's new to EVA altogether-- especially during that scene, where people are most likely to be flailing about unit-01 getting impaled than about kaworu's... attire. but still, do you guys think it was a visual clue that kaworu can synchronize with the eva at will? was it simply a special perk of mark.06, that whoever pilots it doesn't need to wear a headset because the new unit is just that advanced? inb4 "it was a goof"/"anno's going senile"/"there's no headset, so rebuild SUCKS!"

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Jul 26, 2010 1:40 pm Oh, you mean the clips. I feel stupid now. Yeah, Kaworu isn't going to be doing any normal interacting with NERV this time around. Seeing as NERV doesn't really exist anymore, I don't think that will be a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Mon Jul 26, 2010 2:04 pm i'm quite sure they'll have a lot more things to think about it then such as rebuilding the base and hq, damage and casualty assessement, realising a third impact almost just happened wtf is a eva, what is that big lance that saved our lifes, then whoever is piloting the mark 06 doesnt wear a headset. your question assumes seele would hand mark 06 to nerv, and being that they were so secrecy about it, is a big no.

[]:             Quote Post by evaunit13 » Mon Jul 26, 2010 2:16 pm the headset's more important than the plugsuit, right? even when Shinji had to rush when zeruel was attacking, he still wore it. so what would be the point of putting on a plugsuit without a headset.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Mon Jul 26, 2010 2:50 pm I assume he is still an Angel in this version and does not need one, or even be inside.

[]: Re: the interface headset and kaworu's sync with mark.06             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Jul 26, 2010 4:34 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jul 27, 2010 3:12 am [utter speculation]I don't think Seele (or Anno) are attempting to hide it from those "in the know" (Nerv/the audience) but may very well be screwing with the (in-universe) public. From the populace's perspective, Kaworu saved the Earth from Third Impact. Seele may spin it that they stopped Nerv and Gendo from initiating their nefarious plan (to give themselves cover for initiating their nefarious plan). To the people, Mari and Kaworu may be the "good guy" team in opposition to the Nerv weirdos in detention.[/utter speculation] As far as the storytelling goes, Anno never really tried to keep Kaworu's nature a big secret. In his very first scene, the kid is referring to humanity an other, and even has his own name for them. The Director's Cut spells out his identity even further.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 27, 2010 4:06 am I'm not sure but I always thought that those clips were used just to monitor the pilot's brainwaves and they weren't really needed to synchronize, Shinj doesn't have them in EoE yet he was probably synchronized with EVA-01 otherwise he wouldn't have on his own hands the "Holy Stigmata" that EVA-01 received from the EVA Series.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Jul 27, 2010 7:57 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: I'm not sure but I always thought that those clips were used just to monitor the pilot's brainwaves and they weren't really needed to synchronize, Shinj doesn't have them in EoE yet he was probably synchronized with EVA-01 otherwise he wouldn't have on his own hands the "Holy Stigmata" that EVA-01 received from the EVA Series.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 27, 2010 8:11 am Azathoth wrote: Shinji isn't piloting Unit 01 in End of Eva, he's just sitting in it. He does appear to be synchronized to some extent with it - probably not very well, given that the MP Lances go clean through Unit 01's hands and leave only bruiselike marks on Shinji - but he's not controlling anything it's doing. Unit 01 no longer needs a pilot by the time of EoE, she just waits for Shinji because...

[]:             Quote Post by evaunit13 » Tue Jul 27, 2010 9:42 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: He's simply passive and EVA-01 is passive as well, if he wanted he could have done something, as long as he's synchronized he should be able to give commands to the EVA and not only receiving feedbacks from it. Of course there's the matter that EVA-01 can clearly move even without an entry plug since episode 1 of NGE but this is a different point, what I wanted to say is that Shinji is synchronized with the EVA without using those clips thus it's not weird that Kaworu doesn't use them (especially considering that he's probably synchronizing in "his own way" and there's no need to monitor him).

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 27, 2010 10:22 am evaunit13 wrote: but what's strange here is that kaworu doesn't have nerve clips, but still wears a plugsuit, even though the nerve clips are more important.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Jul 27, 2010 10:36 am Clover wrote: Yeah, Kaworu isn't going to be doing any normal interacting with NERV this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Jul 27, 2010 11:00 am carla wrote: i always thought the situation in EoE was a bit different because it wasn't Shinji controlling the eva, it was yui. the syncronization rate is a measure of how well you can control the eva, how efficiently your thought pattern affects the eva's movements (and viceversa). i don't think the headsets are necessarily required for synchronization, but i think they help. in a sense, the headset helps pilots sync because they're essentially forcing their thought pattern onto the not-quite-robotic robot. but if said robot is all up for it, then they don't need as much tech because they're not forcing anything. he was in the eva because yui wanted him to be in the eva, and he probably was partially synched because yui wanted him to be partially synched. he wasn't actively trying. the idea is that the soul in the robot isn't necessarily going to be completely open like that. they go through a lot of trouble to make sure its a compatible soul (the pilot's mother) in the eva to give the pilot the best possible chance, and even then it's not as easy as sitting in the plug and going "hi, i need to fight the angel, could you please move the way i want you to move?"/"sure, my baby." there are obviously some limitations when it comes to actually controlling the eva-- which is what i thik the headset was built for. the difference with yui is that the eva was "awakened" by then, she could just do whatever she wanted to without needing a pilot-- there was something special about her. and similarly, there would be something special about kaworu if he's able to sync without a headset. what that special something is, we presume to know because we've already seen the original, but in-universe the character's don't. the headset could be a big clue for them.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jul 27, 2010 2:45 pm Um, why does she wait for him, actually? Someone must have come up with a theory about this...maybe she wants to make up for being a terrible neglectful mother for most of his life and an incoherent raving cannibal for the last couple months by...uh...putting the fate of the world in his hands in an even more unbelievably traumatic way than ever before? Why does Yui do anything?

[]:             Quote Post by Drew » Wed Jul 28, 2010 2:50 am Of course, anything we say about Q is pretty much speculative, but I believe that Mark-06 is actually Adam itself. With the Key of Nebuchadnezzar replacing embryonic Adam in Rebuild, it seems to be plausible, in which case Kawrou would have no need for A10 clips to synch with his true body. Also, if it is Adam, there is no way in hell SEELE will allow Gendo to even get near the Mark-06. Right now, I'm in doubt that we will see Kawrou even pretend to be just another chosen "Child". He might simply descend to Terminal Dogma immediately.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Jul 28, 2010 9:40 am just to avoid confusion, my initial inquiry wasn't about whether kaworu needs the headset to sync or not-- from that last scene of 2.xx, it seems pretty clear that he doesn't need it. i was just wondering whether his not bothering to wear them makes ultimately obvious what he is, what he can do, or what mark.06, both in-series and people in the audience that are new to EVA. Hyper Shinchan wrote: it's the EVA that allow the pilot to synchronize with her, it's not the pilot that it's forcing himself upon her, otherwise there wouldn't be the need for the compatibility between the pilot and the soul of the EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by Tycho » Wed Jul 28, 2010 6:47 pm just want to note that in the series Kaworu (and SEELE) didn't really try to hide his blue pattern either. He arrives suspiciously. Direct from SEELE. Does what he wants with his synch ratio. Has mees-tee-ree-ous deleted past. He stays in Nerv for like 5 hours, courts Shinji for like 30 minutes, exchanges pleasantries with Rei for 30 seconds and then goes angel on everyone. I'm pretty sure Gendo and Fuyutsuki actually knew it, if they didn't have any actual info, they probably had strong enough suspicions to qualify as "knowledge". This time they have already seen him naked in the friggin' moon... SEELE didn't even try to hide that! I don't even know why they bother to have him inside the plug at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Drew » Wed Jul 28, 2010 10:09 pm It appears that SEELE really didn't care how much Gendo new about their plans using Kawrou in Rebuild. I mean, they easily could have done worse things than just denying Gendo and Fuyutski's shuttle landing clearance on the moon-- and they still allowed them to glance at the incomplete Mark-06 and physics defying Kawrou. It's possible they wanted Gendo to know, so as to force him to show his cards early so to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Jul 29, 2010 2:56 am Drew wrote: It appears that SEELE really didn't care how much Gendo new about their plans using Kawrou in Rebuild. I mean, they easily could have done worse things than just denying Gendo and Fuyutski's shuttle landing clearance on the moon-- and they still allowed them to glance at the incomplete Mark-06 and physics defying Kawrou. It's possible they wanted Gendo to know, so as to force him to show his cards early so to speak.
